citation.sourceDocument: New Jersey Division of Taxation, 2020-and-after gross income tax rate schedules (N.J.S.A. 54A:2-1)
citation.sourceNote: New Jersey is the first seeded state whose graduated tiers differ by filing status. The Single / married-filing-separately schedule (Schedule A) has seven brackets — 1.4% / 1.75% over $20,000 / 3.5% over $35,000 / 5.525% over $40,000 / 6.37% over $75,000 / 8.97% over $500,000 / 10.75% over $1,000,000. The married-filing-jointly / head-of-household / qualifying-surviving-spouse schedule (Schedule B) has eight — it adds a 2.45% tier over $50,000 and a 3.5% tier over $70,000, with 5.525% over $80,000 and 6.37% over $150,000 — so a joint return is taxed more gently in the middle than two single returns. These rates are fixed in statute (N.J.S.A. 54A:2-1) and unchanged since the 2020 addition of the 10.75% 'millionaire's' bracket over $1,000,000. New Jersey has no standard deduction; the personal exemption is $1,000 per filer ($2,000 on a joint return, $1,000 for a head of household), modeled here for the no-dependent filer. The $1,500-per-dependent exemption, the property-tax deduction (up to $15,000) and credit, the medical-expense and other deductions, and the Kansas-City-style local wage taxes (New Jersey has none) are omitted at launch fidelity, so the figure errs slightly high — the conservative side. Verified against the NJ Division of Taxation rate schedules on 2026-06-06.
